The bridge is undoubtedly a famous landmark of the city. 
However, there is really not much to see or do on-site. Because the bridge is located so far outside, it is also not easy to reach, and one must specifically make the journey out of the city, where there is otherwise not much to do. 
We left the bridge relatively quickly and dedicated our remaining time to the very nearby Chinatown.

Brooklyn Bridge is one of the most iconic landmarks in the United States, connecting Manhattan and Brooklyn over the East River. Completed in 1883, it was the first steel-wire suspension bridge in the world and remains a symbol of engineering innovation and New York’s historic growth.

Walking across the bridge is a popular experience, offering stunning views of the Manhattan skyline, the Statue of Liberty in the distance, and the East River below. The pedestrian walkway is especially popular during sunrise and sunset, when the scenery becomes even more picturesque.

A work of art, in every sense! Beautiful, photogenic and useful.
Its impressive presence draws attention from afar...it is very beautiful and all its angles reveal this beauty. 
It generates a visual impact on the landscape that is impossible to ignore: it is there to be used and admired. Impossible not to want to walk or run by it to perceive all its details and admire the panorama.
It gives you a unique perspective of New York or Brooklin at any time of day or night.
